onboarding.miqat_location_denied: Location denied. You can continue but Miqat won't auto-detect.

onboarding.ritual_ifrad_hint: Hajj only — mainly for Saudi residents

damm.body: It appears you crossed the Miqat boundary without being in a state of Ihram. This requires a ruling (Damm — expiation sacrifice) according to Islamic jurisprudence.

tracker_ui.pick_subtitle: Choose a ritual to start counting

IH-01.title: Cutting or Removing Hair
IH-01.content: A person in Ihram should not shave or intentionally remove hair until exiting Ihram.

IH-02.title: Removing Body Hair
IH-02.content: Removing body hair intentionally is not permitted while in Ihram.

IH-03.title: Cutting Nails
IH-03.content: Cutting fingernails or toenails intentionally is not permitted during Ihram.

IH-04.title: Using Perfume
IH-04.content: Applying perfume to the body or clothing after entering Ihram is not allowed.

IH-05.title: Sexual Intercourse
IH-05.content: Sexual intercourse before the first release from Ihram invalidates the Hajj.

IH-06.title: Sexual Intimacy
IH-06.content: Acts of intimacy involving sexual desire should be avoided during Ihram.

IH-07.title: Marriage Contract
IH-07.content: A person in Ihram should not conduct or participate in a marriage contract.

IH-08.title: Hunting Land Animals
IH-08.content: Hunting or assisting in hunting land animals is not permitted during Ihram.

IH-M1.title: Wearing Tailored Clothing
IH-M1.content: Men in Ihram should not wear garments tailored to fit the body such as shirts, trousers, or similar clothing.

IH-M2.title: Covering the Head
IH-M2.content: Men in Ihram should not cover the head with hats, caps, turbans, or similar items.

IH-M3.title: Footwear Covering the Ankles
IH-M3.content: Men in Ihram should normally avoid footwear that covers the ankles. If open sandals are unavailable, some scholars permit wearing shoes out of necessity.

IH-F1.title: Wearing Niqab
IH-F1.content: A woman in Ihram should not wear a niqab.

IH-F2.title: Wearing Gloves
IH-F2.content: A woman in Ihram should not wear gloves.

IH-P1.title: Medical Treatment
IH-P1.content: Medical treatment such as bandaging wounds or taking medicine is permitted during Ihram.

IH-P2.title: Using Shade
IH-P2.content: Using an umbrella, tent, or shade for protection from the sun is permitted.

IH-P3.title: Removing Harmful Insects
IH-P3.content: Removing harmful insects such as lice is permitted.

CM-06.title: Starting Tawaf Incorrectly
CM-06.content: Tawaf should begin in line with the Black Stone.

CM-07.title: Walking Clockwise
CM-07.content: Tawaf must be performed counterclockwise around the Kaaba.

CM-13.title: Tawaf Without Wudu
CM-13.content: Many scholars state that Tawaf should be performed while in a state of ritual purity.

CM-14.title: Starting Sa'i from Marwah
CM-14.content: Sa'i should start from Safa and end at Marwah.

CM-18.title: Leaving Arafat Early
CM-18.content: Pilgrims should remain in Arafat until sunset.

CM-20.title: Incorrect Time for Stoning
CM-20.content: Jamarat al-Aqaba is normally stoned on the Day of Nahr after sunrise. The remaining Jamarat during the Days of Tashriq are stoned after midday.

MH-OBEY.title: Follow Crowd Management Instructions
MH-OBEY.content: Pilgrim movement is regulated to maintain safety and reduce crowding. Following official instructions helps prevent harm.

MA-ENTER-01.title: Enter with the Right Foot
MA-ENTER-01.content: Enter the Masjid with your right foot and say: 'Bismillah, was-salatu was-salamu ala Rasulillah. Allahumma aftah li abwaba rahmatik' (In the name of Allah, and peace and blessings upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, open for me the gates of Your mercy).

MA-TAHIYYA-01.title: Pray Tahiyyat Al-Masjid
MA-TAHIYYA-01.content: Pray two rak'ah (Tahiyyat al-Masjid) upon entering the Masjid before sitting, unless it is a time when prayer is prohibited.

MA-CLEAN-01.title: Maintain Cleanliness
MA-CLEAN-01.content: Keep the Masjid clean. Dispose of all waste in bins, avoid eating inside prayer areas, and arrange your shoes neatly.

MA-VOICE-01.title: Lower Your Voice
MA-VOICE-01.content: Lower your voice inside the Masjid. Loud talking, laughter, and phone calls disturb worshippers and disrespect the sanctity of the place.

MM-SIN-01.title: Sins Carry Greater Weight in Makkah
MM-SIN-01.content: Guard yourself against all sins with extra care in Makkah. While rewards are multiplied in number, wrongdoing carries a greater weight of sin — not in number but in gravity. Allah says: 'Whoever intends to commit wrongdoing therein — We will make him taste a painful punishment.' (Quran 22:25)

MM-GOOD-01.title: Good Deeds Are Multiplied
MM-GOOD-01.content: Prayer in Al-Masjid Al-Haram equals 100,000 prayers elsewhere. Make the most of every moment in worship, dhikr, and supplication.

MM-RESPECT-01.title: Venerate the Symbols of Allah
MM-RESPECT-01.content: Respect and venerate the sacred symbols of Allah. He says: 'Whoever venerates the symbols of Allah — it is from the piety of hearts.' (Quran 22:32)

RA-VIRTUE-01.title: Virtue of the Rawdah
RA-VIRTUE-01.content: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The area between my house and my minbar is a garden from the gardens of Paradise, and my minbar is above my Pool.' (Sahih Al-Bukhari 1196) Pray and supplicate there when you have the opportunity.

RA-NO-PUSH-01.title: Do Not Push or Crowd
RA-NO-PUSH-01.content: Do not push or shove others to reach the Rawdah. The Prophet ﷺ would not want his visitors to harm one another. Full reward is attainable by praying anywhere nearby.

RA-SALAM-01.title: Greeting the Prophet ﷺ
RA-SALAM-01.content: When near the Prophetic Chamber, send salutations in a low, reverent voice: 'As-salamu alayka ya Rasulallah.' Then turn to face the Qibla to make your personal supplication — direct your dua to Allah, not the chamber.

SI-CIRCLE-01.title: Circles of Knowledge and Remembrance
SI-CIRCLE-01.content: Sitting in circles of dhikr and knowledge in the Masjid is among the best of deeds. Do not interrupt or break up a gathering already in progress.

SI-MAKE-SPACE.title: Make Space — A Quranic Command
SI-MAKE-SPACE.content: When asked to make room in a gathering, do so willingly. Allah promises to expand for you in return: your provision, your grave, and your place in Paradise. (Quran 58:11)

SI-NO-RESERVE.title: Do Not Displace Others
SI-NO-RESERVE.content: Do not ask someone to vacate their place so you can sit in it. The Prophet ﷺ forbade this. Whoever leaves their place and returns is still the most deserving of it.

IT-STAY-01.title: I'tikaf — Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ
IT-STAY-01.content: I'tikaf (seclusion in the Masjid for worship) is a confirmed Sunnah, especially in the last ten nights of Ramadan. The Prophet ﷺ observed it every year without exception.

IT-NO-INTIMACY.title: No Intimacy During I'tikaf
IT-NO-INTIMACY.content: Sexual intimacy invalidates I'tikaf. Allah says: 'Do not approach your wives while you are in seclusion in the mosques.' (Quran 2:187)

IT-WORSHIP.title: Fill Your I'tikaf with Worship
IT-WORSHIP.content: During I'tikaf, fill your time with Quran recitation, dhikr, supplication, and learning. Minimise worldly conversation and all distractions.

GA-NO-PUSH.title: No Pushing or Crowding
GA-NO-PUSH.content: Pushing, shoving, and crowding that harms others is forbidden under the foundational principle: 'No harm and no reciprocation of harm.' (Ibn Majah 2341)

GA-MODESTY.title: Modesty in Sacred Spaces
GA-MODESTY.content: Lower your gaze and observe modesty in sacred spaces. Allah commands both men and women to guard their modesty. (Quran 24:30-31)

GA-REMEMBER.title: Remember Allah Abundantly
GA-REMEMBER.content: Fill your visit with the remembrance of Allah. He says: 'Remember Me and I will remember you.' (Quran 2:152) Every moment in the Haram remembered in dhikr is a moment of divine attention.

MM-01.title: Sanctity of Makkah
MM-01.content: One must respect the sanctity of Makkah and avoid sins within it, as Allah promises a painful punishment for those who intend evil therein.

MM-02.title: Prayer in the Holy Mosque
MM-02.content: It is highly recommended to pray frequently in the Haram; one prayer there is better than 100,000 prayers elsewhere.

MM-03.title: Visiting the Prophet's Mosque
MM-03.content: It is Sunnah to visit the Prophet's ﷺ Mosque. Prayer there is better than a thousand prayers in any other mosque except the Holy Mosque.

MM-04.title: Lowering the Voice in the Nabawi Mosque
MM-04.content: It is obligatory to lower one's voice out of respect for the Prophet ﷺ, as commanded in the Quran.

HU-01.title: Entering Ihram from the Miqat
HU-01.content: Anyone intending Hajj or Umrah must enter the state of Ihram at the designated Miqat and should not cross it without doing so.

HU-02.title: Standing at Arafat
HU-02.content: The greatest pillar of Hajj. One must stand at Arafat during the specified time; missing it invalidates the entire Hajj.

HU-03.title: Tawaf al-Ifadah
HU-03.content: A fundamental pillar of Hajj that must be performed after standing at Arafat and Muzdalifah.

HU-04.title: Farewell Tawaf (Wada')
HU-04.content: Obligatory for the pilgrim before leaving Makkah, making the Kaaba the last thing they visit.

IP-01.title: Using an Umbrella
IP-01.content: It is permissible for a person in Ihram to use an umbrella for shade, as the Prophet ﷺ did.

IP-02.title: Bathing in Ihram
IP-02.content: It is permissible to take a bath for cleanliness or to cool down while in Ihram.

IP-03.title: Stitched Clothing for Women
IP-03.content: Women are permitted to wear regular stitched clothing in Ihram, but must avoid the Niqab and gloves.

MA-01.title: Cleanliness of the Mosques
MA-01.content: It is obligatory to keep the Two Holy Mosques clean and avoid littering; mosques are built for worship, not harm.

MA-02.title: Trading in the Mosque
MA-02.content: Buying and selling inside the mosque is discouraged as it is built for prayer and remembrance, not commerce.

MA-04.title: Arriving Early for Prayer
MA-04.content: It is recommended to arrive early to the Haram to secure a place in the front rows and engage in Dhikr.

MA-05.title: Raising the Voice in Dhikr
MA-05.content: It is discouraged to raise one's voice in recitation or supplication if it disturbs other worshippers.

RA-01.title: Prayer in the Rawdah
RA-01.content: It is recommended to pray in the Rawdah; the Prophet ﷺ called it one of the gardens of Paradise.

RA-02.title: Avoiding Harm in the Rawdah
RA-02.content: Pushing or harming others to reach the Rawdah is forbidden. Avoiding harm is a priority over optional prayers.

SI-01.title: Tahiyyat al-Masjid
SI-01.content: A confirmed Sunnah; do not sit upon entering the mosque until you have performed two units of prayer.

SI-02.title: Stepping Over People
SI-02.content: It is forbidden to step over the shoulders of worshippers as it causes harm and discomfort.

SI-03.title: Making Room for Others
SI-03.content: It is recommended to make space for others in gatherings as commanded in the Quran.

SI-04.title: Reserving Places
SI-04.content: It is discouraged to reserve a spot with a prayer mat and leave it unattended for a long period.

IT-01.title: Staying in the Mosque for I'tikaf
IT-01.content: A person in I'tikaf must stay within the mosque and only leave for absolute necessities.

GA-01.title: Lowering the Gaze
GA-01.content: A religious obligation in all places, especially emphasized in the Two Holy Mosques.

GA-02.title: Avoiding Disputes
GA-02.content: Arguing and disputing is forbidden during Hajj, as stated in the Quran.

GA-03.title: Modesty for Women
GA-03.content: Women must adhere to modest Islamic dress and avoid revealing or attracting clothing.

GA-04.title: Refraining from Harm
GA-04.content: A Muslim is one from whose tongue and hand others are safe. Avoid harming pilgrims in word or deed.

etiquette.screen_title: Etiquette of the Two Holy Mosques
etiquette.permits_mistake: If done by ignorance or forgetfulness: no sin or fidya
